description: Minimal Python notes CLI. Serves as a teaching example of Harness Engineering applied to Spec-Driven Development.

features.name: storage_layer
features.title: JSON storage layer
features.description: Atomic read/write of notes from a JSON file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist.
acceptance: src/storage.py exists with load() and save(notes) functions
save() is atomic (write to temp file + rename)
load() returns [] if the file does not exist
tests/test_storage.py covers the three cases above

features.name: note_model
features.title: Note model
features.description: Immutable structure representing a note with id, title, body, created_at.
acceptance: src/notes.py exists with a Note dataclass
Note.new(title, body) generates an incremental id and an ISO 8601 created_at
tests/test_notes.py validates creation and serialization

features.name: cli_add_list
features.title: add and list commands
features.description: Adds a note and lists all stored notes.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli add 'title' --body 'text'` prints the created id
`python -m src.cli list` prints one line per note: `<id>  <created_at>  <title>`
tests/test_cli.py covers both commands using a temp file

features.name: cli_show_delete
features.title: show and delete commands
features.description: Shows and deletes notes by id.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli show <id>` prints title, date and body
`python -m src.cli delete <id>` deletes and confirms with a message
Both commands return exit code != 0 if the id does not exist
tests/test_cli.py covers success and failure

features.name: cli_search
features.title: search command
features.description: Substring search across title or body (case-insensitive).
acceptance: `python -m src.cli search 'word'` lists notes containing the word
If there are no matches, exit code 1 and a clear message
tests/test_cli.py covers match, no-match, and case-insensitivity

features.name: cli_edit
features.title: edit command
features.description: Modifies the title and/or body of an existing note by id.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli edit <id> --title 'new'` updates only the title
`python -m src.cli edit <id> --body 'new'` updates only the body
Passing both flags updates both fields in one call
If no flag is passed, exit code != 0 and a clear message
If the id does not exist, exit code != 0 and a message on stderr
tests/test_cli.py covers success (each flag and combined), missing id, and absence of flags

features.name: cli_recent
features.title: recent command
features.description: Lists the N most recent notes, sorted by created_at descending.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli recent` lists the 5 most recent notes by default
`python -m src.cli recent --limit 10` lets you change the number
Order is by `created_at` from most recent to oldest
Each line follows the format `<id>\t<created_at>\t<title>` (same as `list`)
If there are no notes, exit code 0 and prints nothing (consistent with `list`)
If `--limit` is <= 0, exit code != 0 and a clear message on stderr
tests/test_cli.py covers: default order, custom limit, empty file, invalid limit

features.name: cli_count
features.title: count command
features.description: Shows how many notes are stored.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli count` prints a single integer: the total number of notes
If there are no notes, prints `0`
The command does not modify the notes file
tests/test_cli.py covers: empty file and file with several notes

features.name: cli_export
features.title: export to Markdown
features.description: Exports all notes to a human-readable Markdown file.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli export <path>` writes all notes to `<path>` in Markdown format
Each note appears as a section with a level-2 title (`## <title>`), a line with `created_at`, and the body as a paragraph
Notes are sorted by `id` ascending in the export
If the target path already exists, exit code != 0 and a clear message on stderr (no overwrite by default)
Passing `--force` allows overwriting an existing file
tests/test_cli.py covers: export to new path, overwrite attempt without `--force`, overwrite with `--force`

features.name: cli_stats
features.title: stats command
features.description: Shows aggregate statistics about stored notes.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli stats` prints: total count, oldest note date, newest note date, mean body length (integer, in characters)
Format is one `key: value` per line
If there are no notes, exit code 0 and a single `no notes` message on stdout
The command does not modify the notes file
tests/test_cli.py covers: empty file, file with one note, file with several notes

features.name: cli_clear
features.title: clear command (destructive)
features.description: Removes all notes from the file. Requires explicit confirmation via flag.
acceptance: `python -m src.cli clear` WITHOUT `--yes` prints a confirmation prompt, deletes nothing, and exits with code != 0
`python -m src.cli clear --yes` deletes all notes and confirms with a message like `deleted N notes`
After a successful clear, `list` prints nothing
If the notes file did not exist, `clear --yes` exits with code 0 and reports `deleted 0 notes`
tests/test_cli.py covers: without `--yes` (non-destructive), with `--yes` and notes present, with `--yes` and empty file
